content: The Earth is getting warmer, and this is called climate change. One big reason is something called greenhouse gases that trap heat from the sun. Greenhouse gases are like a big invisible blanket around the Earth that keeps it warm. These gases come from things like cars, factories, and cutting down trees. When cars and factories burn fuel, they send gases like carbon dioxide into the air. Too much carbon dioxide makes the blanket thicker, so more heat gets trapped and the Earth warms up. When the Earth gets too warm, it can cause big storms, melting ice, and hurt animals and plants. People can help by using less fuel, planting trees, and saving energy to keep the Earth cooler. You can help too by turning off lights when you don’t need them and recycling! Together, we can take care of our planet and keep it healthy for all the animals, plants, and people.
